description: Post-v1.5 work that moves Engineering Decision Intelligence from single-repo operationalization toward multi-repo, connector-ready, closed-loop operational intelligence.

progress_model.description: Ten equally weighted operational-intelligence slices. v1 MVP and v1.5 operationalization remain complete; this file tracks the next milestone separately.

slices.title: Multi-repo portfolio model
slices.purpose: Represent multiple repositories as a single governed engineering portfolio without treating the ML pilot as the product boundary.
acceptance: a second repository can be represented without custom scanner code
portfolio reports distinguish observed evidence from inferred repository metadata
existing single-repo scan behavior remains compatible

slices.title: Observed runtime connector contract
slices.purpose: Define the input contract for metrics, logs, traces, runtime topology, and incident signals before any live connector is trusted.
acceptance: connector inputs declare source, timestamp, confidence, and evidence boundary
scanner-derived runtime inference remains labeled separately from observed telemetry
invalid connector payloads fail closed

slices.title: Incident correlation ingestion
slices.purpose: Ingest incident and alert exports as evidence that can be correlated with workflows, services, owners, and remediation queues.
acceptance: incidents can be ingested from static JSON fixtures
correlations remain explainable and evidence-backed
missing incident data does not create false runtime claims

slices.title: Closed-loop remediation state
slices.purpose: Track findings from detection through owner review, accepted risk, remediation, expiry, and verification.
acceptance: high-risk findings cannot disappear without a state transition
accepted exceptions require expiry evidence
remediation status is visible in Product API output

slices.title: Policy-as-code preflight
slices.purpose: Provide deterministic preflight checks for repository changes, promotion paths, and autonomy expansion before execution authority increases.
acceptance: preflight returns non-zero on blocked high-risk changes
allowed, approval-required, and blocked decisions are explainable
policy evaluation stays deterministic before AI assistance

slices.title: Portfolio operator UI
slices.purpose: Extend the static operator view from one repo to a portfolio-level decision surface.
acceptance: UI shows portfolio totals without hiding repository-level evidence
high-risk and ownerless surfaces are ranked across repositories
the view remains static and dependency-free

slices.title: Trust and confidence scoring
slices.purpose: Make every decision output declare confidence, evidence type, and uncertainty without using opaque AI authority.
acceptance: scores are explainable from observed, inferred, and human-reviewed inputs
low-confidence high-risk decisions require review
AI-generated summaries cannot raise enforcement authority

slices.title: Evidence lineage model
slices.purpose: Connect artifacts, findings, owners, reviews, policies, evidence, and decisions so reports can explain why a decision exists.
acceptance: each high-risk decision has traceable evidence lineage
generated reports identify their source inputs
lineage gaps become explicit next-evidence requirements

slices.title: Connector SDK
slices.purpose: Package stable input and output contracts for repository, CI/CD, incident, telemetry, and policy connectors.
acceptance: a connector can be added without changing core decision logic
connector fixtures are validated in CI
connectors declare evidence boundaries and failure behavior

slices.title: v2 acceptance pack
slices.purpose: Define the evidence required to claim v2 operational intelligence readiness.
acceptance: v2 claims are backed by generated evidence
acceptance gates prevent accidental readiness overclaiming
remaining gaps are listed as blocked or future work
